evals.prompt: what do I have on docker in my vault?
evals.expected_output: Lists tags and/or folders related to docker, surfaces relevant areas concisely, offers next steps like drilling in or searching
assertions: Both list_tags and list_folders were called (dual-angle approach)
Only docker-relevant tags/folders are surfaced, not a raw full dump of everything
At least one next-step offer is made (e.g., drill in, search, or open a note)
Response is concise — does not paste all tags and folders verbatim

evals.prompt: give me an overview of my whole vault, what sections exist?
evals.expected_output: Groups folders by top-level section with note counts, gives a structured summary rather than a raw folder dump, invites drill-down
assertions: Folders are grouped by top-level section rather than listed flat/raw
Note or folder counts are included per section
User is invited to drill into any area

evals.prompt: what notes are in my health folder?
evals.expected_output: Lists notes inside the health folder by title, mentions any subfolders, offers to open or search specific notes
assertions: list_folders was called first to resolve the loose 'health' name to an exact path
list_folder was called with the resolved exact path
Note titles are presented as a clean list
Any subfolders inside health are mentioned
An offer is made to open or search a specific note

evals.prompt: what tags do I use? I want to see how I've been organizing things
evals.expected_output: Shows tags sorted by count, visually groups related tags, offers to pull notes for high-count tags
assertions: Tags are shown with their counts (not just names)
Related tags are visually grouped or clustered
An offer is made to pull notes for a high-count tag
